WEAPONS is not uncommon for both private citizens and soldiers to carry weapons All ranged weapons can fire single shot only (as opposed burst, see 118), unless specified otherwise in the weapon description General descriptions of weapons along with ay special (ules are included this section. Equipment ratings range damage and other data for all weapons can be found on the Weapon Table (p. 153}.
Knlte _ Dagger /Bayonet: Found just about anywhere and easily concealed; the knife perfect melee weapon. Some are weighted to deliver maximum hand-to-hand damage while others are balanced for throwing The bayonet is essentially dagger designed attach to the end of rifle. Sword: Once the weapon of choice for the professional dier; the sword is now little more than an elaborate accouter- ment worn with dress uniform given as special gift. tew schools and  societics still teach the art ot swordsmanship, however; and swords are sometimes used in shipboard combat to avoid the chance of breaching the hull,

Vibroblades Though on first glance vibroblade looks Iike bulkier ver- sion of standard bladed weapon; actually high-tech and Iethal. Instead of relying upon tfinely honed edge to make cuts; the blade vibrates at a extremely high frequency, allowing penetrate flesh and most forms of armor The vibration causes distinctive hum that; while often sending an uncontrolled shiv: down those who hear it, will alert a attentive opponent: (Attempting to surprise target with vibroblade attack offers Modifier to the target"$ Perception TN:) Vibroblade: Somewhat larger and heavier than knife , standard vibroblade is still concealable Vibrosword: The vibrosword itself difficult to find_ but it very popular; The Draconis Combine" DEST commandos are trained t0 use variant called vibrokatana and commonly carry them:
Bows Though primitive comparison with other weapons bows are commonly used Many sportsmen prefer to hunt wild game with tneir bows; tew special-operations groups also train with bows Compound Bow: The modern compound bow Is construct- ed with lightweignt alloys and composite materials; series of pulleys maintains stability and increases overall range com pound bow can be loaded ad fired as single Simple Action: Crossbow: While less popular than the compound bow; the crossbow is better at penetrating armor:
